User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the NEU MOA Tracker system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

googleId.description: The Google ID of the user, used for authentication.

role.description: The role of the user (Admin, Faculty, Student).

isBlocked.description: Indicates if the user is blocked from accessing the system.

MemorandumOfAgreement.title: MemorandumOfAgreement
MemorandumOfAgreement.description: Represents a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MemorandumOfAgreement entity.

companyName.description: Name of the company involved in the MOA.

contactPersonEmail.description: Email address of the contact person.

typeOfIndustry.description: Type of industry the company belongs to.

status.description: Current status of the MOA (APPROVED, PROCESSING, EXPIRED, EXPIRING).

isDeleted.description: Indicates if the MOA has been soft deleted.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Represents an audit log entry for user actions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entity.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the action was performed.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Authorization is based on the authenticated user's ID. The `role` field determines access to other parts of the database.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Documents exist only if the user is an admin. Used in rules with `exists()` to grant admin privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the admin user.

definition.description: Stores MOA records. Includes denormalized 'endorsedByCollege' field for authorization independence.

params.name: moaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Memorandum of Agreement.

definition.description: Stores audit logs related to MOA activities. This collection is separate to limit access to only admin users.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: The unique identifier of the audit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support role-based access control and efficient MOA management within the NEU MOA Tracker application. It emphasizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability.

*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles.  Authorization is based on the authenticated user's ID. The `role` field determines access to other parts of the database. No other data is stored here. Access control is simplified to `request.auth.uid == userId`.
*   `/roles_admin/{userId}`: Documents exist only if the user is an admin. Used in rules with `exists()` to grant admin privileges.
*   `/memoranda_of_agreement/{moaId}`: Stores MOA records. To ensure Authorization Independence, the `endorsedByCollege` field is denormalized into each MOA document. This allows faculty to filter MOAs by college without requiring complex `get()` calls in security rules.
*   `/audit_logs/{auditLogId}`: Stores audit logs.  This collection is separate to limit access to only admin users.

**Authorization Independence:**
The design ensures authorization independence by denormalizing the `endorsedByCollege` field within each MOA document. This eliminates the need for security rules to perform `get()` operations on parent documents to determine access, thereby enabling atomic operations and simplifying security rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The segregation of MOAs into a single collection (`/memoranda_of_agreement`) and the denormalization of `endorsedByCollege` ensure that `list` operations are secure. Rules can filter MOAs based on the denormalized `endorsedByCollege` field, without allowing unauthorized access to other MOAs.
*   Admin role is checked via existence in `/roles_admin/{userId}`. This ensures scalable role management.
*   Student role is implicitly granted if they are authenticated and not blocked, so approved MOAs are automatically visible without any further checks.
